prompt: When a new pull request is opened that touches the /migrations directory, automatically post a Slack message to #database-review with the PR title, author, and a reminder to check for backwards compatibility.

Set up an autoresponder for our support email that fires when someone emails on a weekend. It should acknowledge receipt, set expectations for Monday response, and link to our FAQ. Don't fire if the sender is on our VIP list.
When our error rate exceeds 5% for more than 3 minutes, send an alert to PagerDuty with the service name, current error rate, and the top 3 error types. Suppress duplicate alerts for 15 minutes.

Design a webhook handler that posts to our #deployments channel whenever a GitHub release is published. The message should include the version number, release notes summary, and a link to the full changelog.

When a customer's usage exceeds 80% of their plan limit, trigger an in-app notification with their current usage percentage, the limit, and a suggestion to review their plan. Don't fire more than once per week per customer.

Set up a Slack bot response: when someone posts in #incidents with the keyword 'SEV1', the bot should immediately create an incident channel, invite the on-call engineer, and post the incident template.

Configure an out-of-office autoresponder for my Slack DMs. When someone messages me between Dec 23 and Jan 2, it should reply once with my return date and who to contact for urgent issues. Don't auto-reply to bots.
When a new team member is added to our GitHub org, automatically send them a welcome message with links to our dev setup guide, coding standards, and their first-week onboarding checklist.

Design an alert rule: when the disk usage on any server crosses 90%, send a Slack message to #ops with the hostname, current usage, top 5 directories by size, and a suggested cleanup command. Rate-limit to once per hour per host.

When a CI build succeeds on the main branch, post a message to our #releases channel with the build number, commit hash, list of merged PRs since last build, and a one-click deploy button for staging.
